zh.lifeSummary: Nigel de Stafford是Robert de Tosny de Stafford和Adaleza的兒子，於1068年出生在英國萊希斯特斯爾貝爾沃易 卡素。他和Amicia de Ferrers至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1115年在英國德比夏爾德拉可洛福享年47歲，埋葬在英國斯塔福爾伯頓 武朋 特倫特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Stafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name principally from Stafford (Staffordshire), but occasionally from other places with similar names, such as Stafford House in Ifield (Sussex), possibly East and West Stowford in East Down (Devon), and three minor places in Devon called Stafford, in Dolton, Broadhembury, and Colyton parishes. The places are all named with Old English ford ‘ford’ as the final element, but have different initial elements. The Staffordshire placename has Old English stæth ‘river bank, shore’; Stafford in Colyton (Devon) has Old English stān ‘stone, rock’; the Sussex placename has Old English stēor ‘steer, bullock’; East and West Stowford (Devon) have Old English stæf ‘staff, stave, rod’; Stafford in Dolton and Stafford in Broadhembury (Devon) may have Old English stæth, stān, or stæf.

variants.name: Stocker
meanings: German and Swiss German (also Stöcker): topographic name for someone living by a tree stump (see Stock 3) or an occupational name for a tree cutter, from Middle High German stocken ‘to clear land’.
German and Swiss German (also Stöcker); Dutch: occupational name from Middle High German, Middle Dutch stocker ‘jailer’.

English: occupational name from Middle English stokker ‘one who sells stockfish’ (fish dried in the air without salt). This was the usual source of the name in medieval London, where a bylaw of 1419 stated that no stokker should board a ship to buy fish (presumably in order to forestall the market).

nameMeanings.name: Nigel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Anglicized form of the medieval name Nigellus, a Latinized version (ostensibly representing a diminutive of Latin niger ‘black’) of the vernacular Ni(h)el, i.e. Neil . Although it is frequently found in medieval records, this form was probably not used in everyday life before its revival by antiquarians such as Sir Walter Scott in the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: Nigel de Stafford was born in 1068, in Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, England as the son of Robert de Tosny de Stafford and Adaleza. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Amicia de Ferrers. He died in 1115, in Drakelowe, Derbyshire, England, at the age of 47, and was buried in Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Nigel de Stafford est né en 1068, à Belvoir Castle, Leicestershire, Angleterre, fils de Robert de Tosny de Stafford et Adaleza. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Amicia de Ferrers. Il est décédé en 1115, à Drakelowe, Derbyshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 47 ans, et a été enterré à Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, Angleterre.

Er hatte mit Amicia de Ferrers mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1115 im Alter von 47 Jahren in Drakelowe, Derbyshire, England und wurde in Burton upon Trent, Staffordshire, England begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Nigel de Staffordは1068年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアベルボイル カストルでRobert de Tosny de StaffordとAdalezaの息子として生まれた。彼はAmicia de Ferrersとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1115年にイギリスデルブイシュアイアドラケロウで47歳で死去し，イギリススタフォードシュアイアブルトン ウプオン トレントにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Nigel de Stafford(은)는 1068년 영국 레이스스터실르 벨보이르 카스틀에서 Robert de Tosny de Stafford(와)과 Adaleza의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Amicia de Ferrers(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1115년 영국 더비실르 드라켈로에서 47세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스태퍼드실르 버턴 업 트렌트에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Nigel de Stafford родился в 1068 г. в местности белвоир кастл, лисестиршир, Англия. Его родителей звали Robert de Tosny de Stafford и Adaleza. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь от Amicia de Ferrers. Он умер в 1115 г. в местности дрекелоу, дирбишир, Англия в возрасте 47 лет и был похоронен в местности буртон упон трент, стаффордшир, Англия.
